notes: STAR 12-card gravity deck. The plant at 5:34 AM working on Delphine: the small morning weights — compressor short-cycling, fog returning to the glass, the news she has not yet told.

cards.title: THE STOREFRONT GLASS REFOGS
cards.flavor: Two minutes after you wiped it. Always two minutes. The 5 AM dew finding the inside of glass that's six degrees colder than the air.

then.text: You wiped. The fog came back. You wiped. The fog came back. The lesson is the same lesson.

else.text: The glass fogs. You let it. The sky behind the glass starts to be visible.

cards.title: COMPRESSOR ONE SHORT-CYCLES
cards.flavor: Pressure relief on the brine side. Click-click-click. It will, by 6, settle. It always does.

then.text: You walk over. You bleed the line a quarter-turn. The clicking settles.

cards.title: THE NEON ICE BUZZES A CHORD
cards.flavor: C-sharp to D. The transformer is going. You meant to call the sign people last spring. The plant is closing in eleven days; the sign people are not, now, going to come.

cards.title: A CAR DOOR ON CALLIOPE
cards.flavor: Two blocks south. Sound carries this hour. Mr Couvillon parks at 6:09 and walks the half-block.

cards.title: THE BRINE SMELLS LIKE IRON
cards.flavor: Old brine. Old pipes. The smell that's been in this floor since your grandfather. It is the smell of the company.

effects.text: You take it in. Iron and salt. The company's smell.

cards.title: THE OTHER SHIFT IN THE DOCK
cards.flavor: Marcel finishing the closing. He has been here since 10 PM. He is, technically, off in nine minutes. He will, for the next week, find reasons to stay.

cards.title: YOU HAVE NOT CALLED MR COUVILLON
cards.flavor: You meant to last Tuesday. You meant to Monday. He arrives at 6:14 every Wednesday for forty years. The news will reach him in next week's paper unless you reach him first.

cards.title: A KID AT THE GLASS
cards.flavor: Eight, maybe nine. Walking to school early. Has been doing this on Wednesdays since school started. Watches the block come out of the freezer.

cards.title: THE SKY DOES ITS BLUE
cards.flavor: Through the storefront. 5:51 AM is the colour. It is, this morning, that colour because the morning has decided to be it.

effects.text: You stop. You watch the colour. The plant continues.

cards.title: THE PHONE IN THE OFFICE
cards.flavor: Rings twice and stops. The closing notice has been faxed; somebody has read it; somebody is going to ask.

effects.text: Two rings. You don't pick up. The closing is, by now, the thing the room is.

cards.title: YOUR GRANDFATHER'S PEN
cards.flavor: In the punch-card drawer at the counter. The Esterbrook he used to sign the original 1949 lease. You haven't, in fifteen years, picked it up.

cards.title: SIX O'CLOCK
cards.flavor: The wall clock catches the hour and the dock door has not, yet, been rolled up. The day either starts now or it doesn't start.

options.label: Open both doors (cost: 1 Time; gain: −3 FOGGING, set_flag doors_opened_at_six)

effects.text: Dock door rolled up. Storefront unlocked. The morning starts. The neighborhood walks in.

options.label: Stay closed a minute longer (+2 FOGGING)
